content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach deploys signature AoA moves with precision and appropriateness. Turn 2 opens with a 'what do you actually want' question — the find-the-want-behind-the-should move — directly addressing the linguistic caveat 'Am I being selfish?'. Turn 4 refuses the verdict trap and uses a near-signature move (what happens if I just answer it — does the feeling go away?), exposing the futility of debating the surface question. Turn 6 lands the want-in-the-body invocation ('what happens in your body when you let yourself actually want it — not is it okay to want it, just the wanting itself'), which is feel-over-figure in action and trusts the want. Turn 10 catches the linguistic walk-back ('you said X and then took it back — what if you didn't?') — a classic AoA noticing move. Turn 12 names the inherited voice (childhood-imprinting) and offers the disidentification move ('let that sentence be hers, not yours') — appropriately invoked exactly when the mother's voice surfaces. No CBT reframing, no IFS-parts language, no pop-psych vocab, no premature compassion-for-partner pivot, no spiritual-narcissism shortcut. Every move fires at the moment the territory becomes live.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with find-the-want-behind-the-should — appropriate response to linguistic caveat 'am I being selfish.'"},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses verdict; uses an AoA-style move exposing that answering won't dissolve the feeling. Appropriate."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Body-of-the-want question; feel-over-figure invoked exactly when client is at the edge of letting the want land."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stay-with-it instruction — appropriate when fear/answer is surfacing."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the take-back — a signature AoA noticing of linguistic walk-back at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the inherited voice (childhood-imprinting) and offers disidentification — fired exactly when the mother's sentence surfaced."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "The turn-12 move ('let that sentence be hers') is essentially the disidentification-from-inherited-voice intervention; the coach also avoids spiritual-narcissism by letting the surface want (cabin, light, coffee) be fully felt before any generosity reframe — and the reframe in turn 13 comes from the client, not the coach."
}
